USA.current_government: Republican Party; President Donald Trump (second term, 2025–). Controls Senate; House narrow Republican majority.

events.event: Hurricane Katrina devastates Gulf Coast; federal response widely criticized, exposing FEMA failures and deepening racial inequality debate.
Housing bubble collapse begins; subprime mortgage market implodes, triggering the global financial crisis.
Barack Obama elected first African-American president; TARP ($700B bank bailout) signed by outgoing Bush.
American Recovery and Reinvestment Act ($787B stimulus) passed; unemployment peaks near 10%.
Affordable Care Act signed; Tea Party movement surges, Republicans win House in midterms.
Debt-ceiling crisis brings US to brink of default; S&P downgrades US credit rating for first time.
Obama re-elected; Hurricane Sandy causes $65B in damage; fiscal cliff averted at last minute.
Iran nuclear deal (JCPOA) signed; TPP negotiations concluded; Supreme Court legalizes same-sex marriage nationwide.
Donald Trump elected president; Brexit vote shocks global markets; Fed begins rate-hike cycle.
Tax Cuts and Jobs Act reduces corporate tax rate from 35% to 21%; US withdraws from Paris Climate Agreement and TPP.
US-China trade war begins; tariffs on $250B of Chinese goods; steel/aluminum tariffs on allies.
COVID-19 pandemic; CARES Act ($2.2T relief); GDP falls 3.4%; Biden defeats Trump; 230,000 US deaths by election.
January 6 Capitol attack; American Rescue Plan ($1.9T); bipartisan Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act ($1.2T) passed.
Inflation hits 9.1% (40-year high); Fed raises rates 425bps; Inflation Reduction Act ($369B climate/healthcare); Ukraine war support begins.
Debt ceiling crisis resolved at 11th hour; banking stress (SVB/Signature failures); AI boom begins with ChatGPT.
Trump wins 2024 election; inflation cools to ~3%; Fed begins cutting rates; Gaza conflict strains Democratic coalition.
DOGE (Department of Government Efficiency) launches mass federal workforce cuts; broad tariffs imposed on trading partners; NATO commitment questioned.

CHN.current_government: Chinese Communist Party; General Secretary and President Xi Jinping (third term since 2022, no term limit).

events.event: China's 11th Five-Year Plan prioritizes balanced growth, energy efficiency, and reducing rural-urban inequality; GDP growth 11.4%.
Beijing Olympics showcases China's rise; $586B stimulus launched after global financial crisis; Tibet protests suppressed.
China overtakes Japan as world's second-largest economy; Google withdraws from China over censorship and hacking.
Xi Jinping becomes CCP General Secretary; Bo Xilai scandal exposes factional power struggles.
Belt and Road Initiative announced; Shanghai Free Trade Zone launched; Xi begins anti-corruption campaign (1M+ officials eventually disciplined).
Stock market crashes 30% in two months; yuan devalued 3%; One Child Policy replaced with Two Child Policy.
Xi Jinping Thought enshrined in CCP constitution; 19th Party Congress consolidates Xi's power; South China Sea militarization accelerates.
National People's Congress removes presidential term limits; US-China trade war begins with $34B tariffs.
Hong Kong protests erupt over extradition bill; Huawei banned from US supply chains; GDP slows to 6.1%.
COVID-19 pandemic originates; strict zero-COVID lockdowns contain initial spread; Hong Kong National Security Law imposed.
Common Prosperity campaign targets wealth inequality; tech crackdown (Alibaba $2.8B fine, DiDi delisted, gaming restrictions); Evergrande collapses.
Xi Jinping's third term confirmed; zero-COVID protests force policy reversal; real estate crisis deepens.
Post-COVID recovery disappoints; youth unemployment hits 21.3% (data suppressed after); deflation concerns; record $823B trade surplus.
Major stimulus package launched ($1.4T+) to combat deflation and property crisis; Taiwan Strait tensions escalate; BRI expands to 140+ countries.
US tariffs escalate to 145%+; China retaliates; decoupling accelerates; GDP growth target ~5% amid structural headwinds.

RUS.current_government: President Vladimir Putin (fifth term since May 2024, in power since 2000 except 2008–2012). Single-party dominance of United Russia.

events.event: Russia-Ukraine gas dispute; Kremlin tightens state control over energy sector (Yukos/Khodorkovsky case completed).
Putin's Munich Security Conference speech denounces US 'unipolar world'; Russia suspends CFE arms treaty.
Russia-Georgia War: Russia recognizes South Ossetia and Abkhazia; global financial crisis causes 7.8% GDP contraction.
Medvedev presidency (Putin as PM); Russia-NATO 'reset' with Obama; gas supply to Europe briefly cut.
Putin returns to presidency; massive protests in Moscow suppressed; 'foreign agents' law targets NGOs.
Annexation of Crimea; war in Donbas begins; Western sanctions imposed; ruble collapses 50%; oil price halves.
Russia intervenes militarily in Syria supporting Assad; S&P downgrades Russia to junk; Minsk II ceasefire signed.
Alleged interference in US election; Russia excluded from G8; import-substitution policies accelerate.
Putin re-elected with 77%; Skripal poisoning leads to mass diplomat expulsions; pension reform sparks rare mass protests.
Constitution amended to allow Putin to rule until 2036; COVID pandemic; oil price war with Saudi Arabia crashes prices.
Navalny imprisoned after poisoning; Russia masses troops on Ukraine border; NS2 pipeline completed.
Full-scale invasion of Ukraine; unprecedented Western sanctions freeze $300B in reserves; Finland and Sweden apply for NATO.
Wagner mutiny (Prigozhin killed); war of attrition in Ukraine; 'war economy' drives 3.6% GDP growth; military budget reaches 6% of GDP.
Putin wins fifth term (87%); Ukraine strikes deep into Russia; North Korean troops deployed to Russia; EU/US escalate weapons supplies.
Ceasefire negotiations begin under US pressure; Russian economy strained by sanctions and military spending; dollar reserves partially restored via China/India trade.

IND.current_government: Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP)-led NDA coalition; PM Narendra Modi (third term since June 2024, though BJP fell short of majority alone).

events.event: NREGA enacted: guarantees 100 days of paid rural employment per household; landmark social protection.
Mumbai 26/11 attacks kill 166; India-Pakistan relations freeze; US-India Civil Nuclear Agreement signed.
UPA wins second term; Right to Education Act passed.
Anna Hazare anti-corruption movement; India's population crosses 1.2B; GDP growth at 6.6% after strong decade.
Modi/BJP wins landslide majority (282/543 seats); 'Make in India' and 'Swachh Bharat' initiatives launched.
Surgical strikes across LoC in Kashmir after Uri attack; November demonetization of Rs. 500/1000 notes disrupts economy.
GST (Goods and Services Tax) implemented July 1, unifying India's fragmented indirect tax system.
BJP wins 303 seats; Article 370 revoked in Jammu & Kashmir, stripping special status; Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA) passed, sparking protests.
COVID-19: world's strictest lockdown announced with 4 hours notice; economy contracts 5.8%; Galwan border clash with China kills 20 soldiers; Huawei and 200+ Chinese apps banned.
Second COVID wave devastates India (Delta variant); farmers' protest against three farm laws forces repeal by Modi government.
India remains 'neutral' on Ukraine war, continues buying discounted Russian oil; becomes world's fifth largest economy (overtaking UK).
G20 presidency; Chandrayaan-3 soft-lands near Moon's south pole; India overtakes China as world's most populous country (1.44B).
2024 general election: BJP wins 240 seats (below majority alone); NDA coalition government formed; Ram Mandir consecrated in Ayodhya.
India-China border normalization talks advance; India-Pakistan tensions simmer; 'Viksit Bharat' (Developed India by 2047) roadmap published; GDP growth ~6.5%.

GBR.current_government: Labour Party; Prime Minister Keir Starmer (since July 2024, 412/650 seat majority).

events.event: 7/7 London bombings kill 52; UK hosts G8 at Gleneagles; Blair wins third term.
Gordon Brown becomes PM; Northern Rock becomes first UK bank run in 150 years; starts Great Recession in UK.
UK bank bailouts: RBS and Lloyds partly nationalised; GDP shrinks 4.2% in 2009.
Conservative-Lib Dem coalition under Cameron begins austerity; VAT raised to 20%; public spending cut 25%.
London Olympics; Scotland independence referendum announced; austerity cuts deepen.
Scotland votes 55–45 to remain in UK; Conservative majority government elected 2015.
Brexit referendum: 52% vote Leave; Cameron resigns; Theresa May becomes PM; pound falls 15%.
Boris Johnson becomes PM with 'Get Brexit Done' mandate; wins 80-seat majority; Withdrawal Agreement signed.
UK formally leaves EU; COVID-19: 130,000 UK deaths; furlough scheme protects 11M jobs; vaccine rollout leads the world.
UK-EU Trade and Cooperation Agreement takes effect; COP26 hosted in Glasgow; supply chain crisis, fuel shortages.
Johnson resigns over Partygate; Liz Truss 'mini-budget' crashes pound and gilt markets; Sunak becomes PM; inflation hits 11.1%.
UK joins CPTPP trade bloc; Windsor Framework resolves NI Brexit dispute; strikes across public sector over pay.
Labour wins landslide election (412 seats); Keir Starmer becomes PM; pledges to repair EU relations and build 1.5M homes.
UK-EU summit on defence and trade cooperation; NHS crisis deepens; growth plan announced with planning reforms.

DEU.current_government: CDU/CSU-SPD grand coalition; Chancellor Friedrich Merz (CDU, since March 2025).

events.event: Angela Merkel becomes Germany's first female Chancellor heading CDU/CSU-SPD grand coalition.
Germany's export-led economy hit by global crisis; Merkel government's €500B bank guarantee stabilizes system.
German-led EU austerity imposed on Greece; Germany leads eurozone crisis management; 'Schuldenbremse' (debt brake) enshrined in Basic Law.
Fukushima triggers German nuclear phase-out decision (Energiewende); last nuclear plants to close by 2022.
Germany accepts 890,000 asylum seekers ('Wir schaffen das'); Volkswagen Dieselgate scandal; refugee crisis reshapes politics.
AfD surges in state elections; Berlin truck attack; Brexit shakes EU unity.
SPD joins another grand coalition; IG Metall wins 28-hour week; Nord Stream 2 construction begins.
Merkel retires after 16 years; SPD's Olaf Scholz leads 'traffic light' coalition (SPD-Greens-FDP).
Russia invades Ukraine; Germany's Zeitenwende (historic foreign policy U-turn): €100B defence fund; gas dependency exposed; LNG terminals fast-tracked.
Germany enters recession (-0.3%); last nuclear plants shut; coalition disputes over debt brake paralyse government.
Scholz coalition collapses over budget dispute; snap elections called; AfD polls second nationally; Germany's industrial competitiveness crisis.
CDU/CSU-SPD coalition formed under Merz; debt brake reformed to allow defence spending; Bundeswehr modernisation accelerates.

FRA.current_government: Emmanuel Macron (President, third term unlikely — constitutionally barred after 2027); PM François Bayrou leads centrist coalition since Jan 2025.

events.event: French suburbs riots (banlieues) expose integration failures; French voters reject EU Constitution in referendum.
Nicolas Sarkozy elected on modernisation platform; begins labour market reforms.
France takes G8/EU presidency during financial crisis; Sarkozy brokers EU response.
François Hollande elected on anti-austerity platform; 75% top tax rate briefly introduced; Mali intervention.
Charlie Hebdo and November Paris attacks kill 147; state of emergency declared; France joins Syria air campaign.
Emmanuel Macron elected president (En Marche!); defeats Le Pen 66–34%; La République En Marche wins legislative majority.
Gilets jaunes (Yellow Vests) protests over fuel taxes and inequality; Macron makes €10B concessions.
COVID-19 kills 60,000+ by year end; France receives €40B from EU Recovery Fund.
Macron re-elected over Le Pen (58–42%); loses legislative majority; pension reform raised retirement age to 64.
Pension reform passed via Article 49.3 (bypassing parliament); nationwide strikes and protests; New Caledonia unrest.
Snap elections called after EU vote rout; hung parliament; three PMs in one year; Paris Olympics held successfully.
Bayrou government focuses on deficit reduction (6.1% of GDP); France leads European defence spending push; nuclear energy investment.

JPN.current_government: Liberal Democratic Party (LDP); PM Sanae Takaichi (since late 2024 LDP leadership election — or PM Ishiba; LDP in coalition with Komeito).

events.event: Shinzo Abe becomes PM (first term); constitutional revision pushed; GDP growth modest at 2.0%.
DPJ ends 54 years of LDP dominance; Yukio Hatoyama PM; Japan's Lost Decade deepens.
Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami: 19,000 dead; Fukushima nuclear disaster triggers all reactor shutdowns; JPY surges to 76/$.
Abe returns to power with Abenomics: 3 arrows (monetary easing, fiscal spending, structural reform); LDP wins landslide.
Bank of Japan launches unprecedented QE (60–70T yen/year); Abe visits Yasukuni Shrine straining China/Korea ties.
Abe reinterprets constitution to allow collective self-defence; Japan's population falls for fifth consecutive year.
Bank of Japan introduces negative interest rates; Abe and Obama visit Pearl Harbor and Hiroshima; Japan joins Trans-Pacific Partnership.
Japan-Korea trade dispute escalates; Reiwa era begins as Emperor Naruhito ascends; consumption tax raised to 10%.
COVID-19 causes 2020 Olympic postponement; GDP falls 4.3%; Abe resigns citing health; Suga becomes PM.
Tokyo Olympics held behind closed doors; Kishida Fumio becomes PM; Japan considers first strike capability.
Abe assassinated; Japan doubles defence budget to 2% of GDP by 2027; Japan joins G7 Russia sanctions.
Japan issues 40-year bonds to fund defence; BOJ hints at policy pivot; inflation above 3% for first time in decades.
BOJ raises rates for first time since 2007 (0.25%); yen briefly at 160/$; Kishida resigns; LDP leadership election.
Japan deepens security ties with USA, Australia, Philippines vs. China; wage growth sustains at 5%+; ageing crisis worsens.

BRA.current_government: Workers' Party (PT); President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (third term since January 2023).

events.event: Mensalão scandal: Lula's PT involved in vote-buying scheme; PT loses credibility but Lula survives.
Lula re-elected; pre-salt oil discovery announced (massive offshore reserves); Bolsa Família expands.
Brazil gains investment grade rating; joins BRICS; financial crisis impact is mild — 'Brazil didn't need the lifeboat'.
Dilma Rousseff elected; Brazil grows 7.5% (fastest in 25 years); World Cup and Olympics host rights secured.
Mass protests (Jornadas de Junho): millions on streets over corruption and public service costs.
Lava Jato (Car Wash) operation begins exposing Petrobras corruption ($2B+); Brazil hosts World Cup amid protests; recession begins.
Dilma Rousseff impeached over fiscal accounting; Michel Temer takes over; Olympics held in Rio; austerity measures.
Jair Bolsonaro elected on anti-corruption, anti-PT platform; real falls; Lula imprisoned on corruption charges.
Amazon deforestation surges 30%; Brazil withdraws from Paris Agreement commitments; pension reform passes.
Brazil suffers 2nd highest COVID deaths (600,000+); Bolsonaro dismisses pandemic; GDP falls 4.1%.
Lula defeats Bolsonaro 50.9–49.1% in closest election ever; Bolsonaro supporters storm congress in January 2023.
Lula reorients foreign policy (BRICS, Global South, Amazon protection); fiscal framework passes markets; deforestation drops 50%.
Brazil assumes G20 presidency; BRL weakens on fiscal concerns; COP30 awarded to Belém; Brazil leads Ukraine peace talks.
COP30 preparations in Amazonia; fiscal deficit concerns; China remains top trade partner; Brazil pushes for BRICS expansion.

AUS.current_government: Australian Labor Party; Prime Minister Anthony Albanese (second term since May 2025 election).

events.event: Kevin Rudd wins ending 11 years of Howard Liberal rule; Kyoto Protocol ratified; apology to Stolen Generations.
Australia avoids recession (only developed nation to do so) thanks to China commodity demand and $42B stimulus.
Julia Gillard deposes Rudd; minority government with Greens; resources super-profits tax dispute.
Tony Abbott wins on axing carbon tax; 'Stop the Boats' asylum policy; Rudd briefly returns then Labor loses.
Carbon tax repealed; Australia joins US-led airstrikes in Iraq/Syria; MH370 and MH17 crises affect Australia.
Malcolm Turnbull replaced by Scott Morrison; Chinese influence concerns grow; same-sex marriage legalised.
Black Summer bushfires (33 dead, 18M hectares burned); COVID managed early with state lockdowns; China trade dispute begins.
AUKUS security pact announced (nuclear submarines with US/UK); China trade war escalates; vaccine rollout delayed.
Anthony Albanese elected; climate legislation (43% reduction by 2030); China ties slowly repair.
Voice to Parliament referendum defeated; AUKUS submarine deal progresses; interest rates peak at 4.35%.
Australia pushes for Pacific climate finance; housing affordability crisis; immigration hits record 518,000.
Labor re-elected; net zero by 2050 remains target; AUKUS Virginia-class subs timeline firmed; cost of living central issue.

CAN.current_government: Liberal Party (minority) under PM Mark Carney since March 2025; federal election expected mid-2025 likely bringing Conservatives under Pierre Poilievre.

events.event: Stephen Harper wins minority Conservative government ending 13 years of Liberal rule.
Harper wins majority; Canada's housing-backed mortgages crisis averted by regulation; commodity boom.
Justin Trudeau wins majority on 'sunny ways' platform; Canada 2030 climate plan; Syrian refugee intake (25,000).
Carbon price introduced; Trans Mountain pipeline approved; CETA with EU ratified.
USMCA replaces NAFTA; legalization of recreational cannabis; steel tariff fight with US.
Trudeau re-elected minority; SNC-Lavalin scandal; US-Canada relations tense over tariffs.
COVID-19: CERB income support ($2,000/month) reaches 9M Canadians; WE Charity scandal; border closed with US.
Trudeau wins snap election (another minority); vaccine mandate for federal workers; COP26 commitment.
Freedom Convoy blocks Ottawa; Emergencies Act invoked (first time); inflation hits 8.1%; Bank of Canada raises rates 400bps.
Canada-India diplomatic crisis (Sikh leader assassination); wildfires burn 18M hectares; housing crisis worsens.
Trudeau faces caucus revolt; NDP withdraws supply agreement; Chrystia Freeland resigns; Conservatives lead polls by 20pts.
Mark Carney becomes PM; Trump threatens 25% tariffs and annexation rhetoric; Canada responds with counter-tariffs and trade diversification.

KOR.current_government: People Power Party (PPP); President Yoon Suk-yeol impeached December 2024; acting President Han Duck-soo; presidential election pending 2025.

events.event: Ban Ki-moon becomes UN Secretary-General; Korea-US FTA negotiations begin.
Global financial crisis; Lee Myung-bak (conservative) elected; Korean exports contract sharply.
KORUS FTA signed; Cheonan naval vessel sunk by North Korea (46 dead); Yeonpyeong island shelling.
Park Geun-hye becomes first female president; tensions with Japan over comfort women and Dokdo.
Park Geun-hye impeached over Choi Soon-sil scandal; millions protest in candlelight revolution; THAAD deployment agreed.
Moon Jae-in elected; Korean détente with North Korea; China retaliates economically over THAAD.
Panmunjom Declaration (Moon-Kim summit); Trump-Kim Singapore summit; Winter Olympics diplomacy.
Japan-Korea trade war: Japan restricts chip materials; Korea removes Japan from white list; GSOMIA almost cancelled.
K-Culture (BTS, Squid Game) global dominance; IPEF membership; semiconductor dominance (Samsung, SK Hynix).
Yoon Suk-yeol (conservative) narrowly elected; Korea joins Chip 4 Alliance; North Korea fires record 37 missiles.
Korea-Japan relations normalised (Camp David trilateral summit); birth rate falls to 0.72 (world lowest).
Yoon declares then quickly reverses martial law (December 3); impeached by National Assembly; political crisis.
Presidential election; economic uncertainty; North Korea-Russia arms deal; semiconductor competition with TSMC intensifies.

MEX.current_government: Morena party; President Claudia Sheinbaum (since October 2024, first female president, won with 59% of vote).

events.event: Felipe Calderón wins disputed election; launches 'war on drugs' deploying military against cartels.
Drug violence surges: 6,837 killed; financial crisis hits Mexico hard (GDP falls 6% in 2009).
PRI returns to power with Enrique Peña Nieto; structural reforms: energy, telecom, education, finance.
Energy reform opens oil sector to foreign investment after 75 years of Pemex monopoly.
43 Ayotzinapa student teachers disappear; government response fuels national crisis.
Major earthquakes kill 300+ in Mexico City; NAFTA renegotiation begins.
AMLO (López Obrador) wins 53%; begins 'Fourth Transformation'; USMCA agreed replacing NAFTA.
AMLO cancels $13B new Mexico City airport; homicide rate hits record 34,582; nearshoring boom begins.
COVID kills 190,000+ officially (true toll estimated 3×); GDP falls 8.3%; remittances hit record $40B.
Mexico surpasses China as top US import source (nearshoring); energy reform defeated; inflation 8.7%.
Mexico becomes largest trade partner to US; Jalisco and Sinaloa cartels at war; fentanyl crisis strains US relations.
Claudia Sheinbaum elected; judicial reform passes (judges directly elected); Trump threatens 25% tariffs.
Negotiating USMCA review under Trump tariff threats; nearshoring investment continues; security cooperation with US tense.

IDN.current_government: Gerindra-led coalition; President Prabowo Subianto (since October 2024, won with 58.6%); ex-general who ran against Jokowi twice.

events.event: Indian Ocean tsunami recovery continues; Aceh peace deal signed ending 30-year separatist conflict.
Indonesia resilient in global crisis; Islamic banking grows; GDP maintains 6%+ growth.
Susilo Bambang Yudhoyono re-elected; Indonesia joins G20; counter-terrorism successes.
Joko Widodo (Jokowi) elected — first non-elite, non-military president; infrastructure push begins.
Blasphemy conviction of Jakarta's first Chinese-Christian governor Ahok inflames identity politics.
Jokowi re-elected; capital relocation from Jakarta to Nusantara (Borneo) announced; Papua unrest.
COVID kills 57,000+ officially; Omnibus Job Creation Law passes amid strikes; nickel export ban extended.
Indonesia hosts G20; nickel refining boom (EV battery supply chain); palm oil export ban causes global food crisis.
Indonesia becomes world's largest nickel producer; general election campaign begins; Nusantara capital construction.
Prabowo Subianto wins election with Jokowi's son as VP candidate; concerns over democratic backsliding.
Indonesia deepens ties with China (BRI) while maintaining US defence relationship; food sovereignty programme; aims for $1T GDP by 2030.

TUR.current_government: Justice and Development Party (AKP); President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan (in power since 2003, executive presidency since 2018, re-elected 2023).

events.event: EU accession talks officially open; Turkey's economy booms averaging 6% growth 2002–2008.
AKP wins 47% in elections; Erdoğan allies with Gülen movement; military power constrained.
Constitutional referendum passed weakening military courts; Gaza flotilla raid strains Israel ties.
Gezi Park protests: largest anti-government demonstrations; Erdoğan calls protesters 'çapulcuları'; Gülen split begins.
HDP enters parliament (13%); peace process with Kurds collapses; ISIS attack in Suruç; Russia jet shot down.
Failed military coup (July 15); 250+ killed; Gülen movement designated terrorist (FETÖ); 150,000+ arrested/fired.
Constitutional referendum: executive presidency system passes narrowly (51.4%); EU accession talks effectively frozen.
Turkey buys Russian S-400 missiles; expelled from F-35 program; military operation in northern Syria (Olive Branch, Peace Spring).
Inflation spirals to 85% as Erdoğan refuses rate hikes; lira loses 44% in one year.
Kahramanmaraş earthquake kills 50,000+; Erdoğan narrowly wins re-election (52%) despite poor response; inflation briefly 85%.
CHP wins municipal elections in Istanbul, Ankara, İzmir; lira stabilises as orthodox monetary policy adopted; IMF programme.
Turkey mediates Russia-Ukraine talks; plays key NATO role while maintaining Russia ties; EU sanctions waiver sought for trade.

SAU.current_government: Absolute monarchy; King Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud (since 2015); Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman (MBS) de facto ruler as PM since 2022.

events.event: King Abdullah takes throne; oil at $60/barrel and rising; women granted right to vote in municipal elections (first phase).
Oil hits $147/barrel; Saudi current account surplus reaches $132B; sovereign wealth fund SIF expands.
Arab Spring: Saudi Arabia sends troops to Bahrain to suppress protests; $130B domestic social spending pledge.
Oil price collapses from $115 to $45; Saudi Arabia increases production (OPEC war for market share against US shale).
King Salman accedes; Saudi-led coalition launches Yemen war (Operation Decisive Storm); Vision 2030 framework begins.
Vision 2030 launched by MBS: diversify economy from oil; Saudi Aramco IPO planned; Ritz-Carlton anti-corruption detentions.
MBS named Crown Prince; women granted right to drive; movie cinemas reopened after 35 years; Qatar blockade.
Jamal Khashoggi murdered in Istanbul consulate; international condemnation; Saudi Aramco IPO delayed; women begin driving.
COVID collapses oil demand; second oil price war (with Russia); Saudi GDP falls 4.1%; VAT tripled to 15%.
Oil revenues soar on Ukraine-driven price spike; OPEC+ cuts production defying US pressure; Saudi-Iran rapprochement via China.
Saudi-Iran diplomatic normalisation (China-brokered); Yemen ceasefire; Aramco invests in Chinese refining; BRICS invited to join.
Hosting COP16 biodiversity; NEOM megacity behind schedule; Vision 2030 targets revised; oil revenues under pressure at $80/barrel.
Normalisation with Israel stalled by Gaza war; continued LIV Golf/sports-washing strategy; oil production capacity 12.5Mbpd.

ZAF.current_government: Government of National Unity (GNU); President Cyril Ramaphosa (ANC) continues as president but ANC governs with DA and other parties after losing majority in 2024.

events.event: South Africa hosts 2010 World Cup bid won; land redistribution debate intensifies; AIDS crisis: 5.5M infected.
Jacob Zuma defeats Thabo Mbeki in ANC leadership election at Polokwane.
Mbeki ousted by ANC; global crisis plus power cuts (load-shedding) cause GDP to stall; xenophobic violence.
FIFA World Cup hosted successfully; South Africa joins BRICS.
Marikana massacre: 34 platinum miners shot by police; Zuma corruption scandals multiply.
#FeesMustFall protests; Zuma fires finance minister (rand falls 9% in one day).
S&P and Moody's downgrade SA to junk; 'state capture' inquiry begins exposing Zuma-Gupta network.
Cyril Ramaphosa replaces Zuma as president; 'New Dawn' declared; land reform debate intensifies.
July unrest (Zuma jailing riots): 350+ dead, $3.4B damage; COVID third wave; rolling blackouts become permanent feature.
Load-shedding reaches Stage 6 (12hrs/day); SA refuses to condemn Russia's Ukraine invasion at UN; 33% unemployment rate.
BRICS summit in Johannesburg (SA hosts); US warns of arms to Russia; Ramaphosa survives Phala Phala scandal.
ANC loses majority for first time since 1994 (40%); Government of National Unity formed with DA; load-shedding ends temporarily.
GNU tests coalition management; energy plan improves via private generation; G20 presidency; ICJ genocide case against Israel strains US ties.

NGA.current_government: All Progressives Congress (APC); President Bola Tinubu (since May 2023).

events.event: Nigeria reaches debt relief agreement with Paris Club ($18B); Obasanjo government; oil revenues boom.
Umaru Yar'Adua elected (disputed); Niger Delta militancy surges, cutting oil output 25%.
Boko Haram insurgency begins in Maiduguri; central bank doubles capital requirements saving banks.
Goodluck Jonathan elected; post-election violence kills 800; subsidy removal riots; Boko Haram attacks escalate.
Nigeria rebases GDP: overtakes South Africa as continent's largest economy; Boko Haram kidnaps 276 Chibok schoolgirls.
Muhammadu Buhari wins — first peaceful democratic transfer of power; Boko Haram partially pushed back; oil crash hits revenues.
Recession (-1.6%): oil collapse, forex crisis, CBN fixes naira; food inflation surges.
#EndSARS protests against police brutality; Lekki Tollgate killings; COVID-19; oil revenues crash.
Twitter/X banned for 7 months after deleting Buhari's post; insecurity worsens across Nigeria; inflation 18%.
Tinubu elected; naira devalued, fuel subsidy removed (from ₦184 to ₦600+/litre); inflation hits 33%; currency crashes 70%.
Mass 'hunger protests' across Nigeria; inflation peaks 35%; CBN raises rates to 26.75%; naira briefly stabilises.
Nigeria pushes for ECOWAS cohesion after Niger/Mali/Burkina coups; oil theft reduction; IMF programme discussions; LNG expansion.

PAK.current_government: Pakistan Muslim League-N (PML-N) led coalition; PM Shehbaz Sharif (second term since February 2024 election); Asif Zardari as President. Military retains dominant behind-the-scenes role.

events.event: Kashmir earthquake kills 73,000; Musharraf military regime; US aid to Pakistan surges post-9/11.
Benazir Bhutto returns and is assassinated; Emergency Rule declared by Musharraf; lawyers' movement.
PPP wins elections; Musharraf resigns; Asif Zardari becomes president; Mumbai 26/11 strains India ties; financial crisis hits.
Osama bin Laden killed in Abbottabad; US-Pakistan relations crater; Raymond Davis affair; Salala checkpoint attack.
PML-N wins; Nawaz Sharif PM (first democratic transfer of power); CPEC ($46B China-Pakistan Economic Corridor) agreed.
Panama Papers implicate Nawaz Sharif; CPEC construction accelerates; TTP insurgency worsens.
Imran Khan wins election with military backing; Nawaz Sharif jailed; PTI government begins.
Afghanistan falls to Taliban; 3M+ Afghan refugees in Pakistan; PTI government economic crisis deepens.
Imran Khan removed via no-confidence vote; catastrophic floods cover 1/3 of country, 1,700 dead; IMF bailout ($6B).
Imran Khan arrested, convicted; PTI banned; economy in freefall (inflation 38%); Shehbaz Sharif PM caretaker.
Elections held; PTI wins most seats but military-backed coalition installed; Imran Khan remains imprisoned.
IMF extended fund facility; India-Pakistan tensions over Kashmir re-escalate; CPEC second phase negotiations; TTP attacks from Afghanistan.

UKR.current_government: Servant of the People party; President Volodymyr Zelensky (since April 2019; election postponed due to war under martial law).

events.event: Orange Revolution outcome: Yushchenko wins re-run presidential election over Yanukovych.
Russia-Georgia war warns Ukraine; NATO membership action plan denied at Bucharest summit.
Yanukovych wins presidential election; Ukraine declared 'non-bloc' (no NATO); Russia lease on Sevastopol naval base extended to 2042.
Euromaidan begins: protests over Yanukovych's EU Association Agreement rejection in favour of Russia.
Yanukovych flees; Russia annexes Crimea; Donbas war begins; IMF $17B programme; economy contracts 10%.
Minsk II agreements; continued Donbas conflict; Ukraine-EU DCFTA enters force; anti-corruption reforms begin.
Zelensky elected 73% on anti-corruption platform; impeachment inquiry into Trump over Ukraine arms call.
Russia masses 100,000 troops on Ukraine border; Zelensky pushes for NATO MAP; US warns of imminent invasion.
Full-scale Russian invasion (Feb 24): Ukraine defends Kyiv, recaptures Kherson and Kharkiv oblasts; $100B+ in Western military/economic aid.
Ukrainian counter-offensive falls short; deep strikes into Russia using drones; NATO membership promised 'when conditions allow'; economy grows 5.3%.
Russia captures Avdiivka; North Korean troops join Russian forces; Trump's election clouds US support; F-16s arrive.
Ceasefire negotiations under Trump pressure; territorial concessions discussed; Ukraine demands security guarantees; reconstruction plan ($500B) published.
